channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Fortnite Teases Crossover with Super Meat Boy 3D
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has a new crossover on the horizon, and it is with none other than Team Meat’s upcoming title, Super Meat Boy 3D.

The upcoming title was revealed during last year’s Xbox Games Showcase and is scheduled to release on consoles and PC in 2026. It seems the game’s release date was revealed early, but we have yet to receive official confirmation. However, we now know with whom the game’s first collaboration will be.

Fortnite x Super Meat Boy 3D Coming Soon

The collaboration between Fortnite and Super Meat Boy 3D was confirmed in a post on the official Epic Games Store X account (formerly Twitter). However, no specific details about this crossover were shared; the post featured only the key art for Team Meat’s upcoming game, which displayed Fortnite’s logo on the side. The caption offered no hints, consisting solely of three steak emojis.

Based on past collaborations, there are various ways Fortnite could approach this crossover. Players should expect cosmetics for sure, as that is a part of almost every collaboration. Other than that, we could see a change towards Fortnite island, a new POI, maybe new abilities, or mythics. However, this is just speculation at the moment, and we will only know for sure when the official announcement arrives.

In other news, Fortnite’s PvE Save the World mode will become free-to-play for all players in April 2026. Additionally, the developers have announced changes to the prices of V-Bucks and Battle Passes. What do you think the Fortnite x Super Meat Boy 3D collaboration will entail? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Marvel MaXimum Collection releasing for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, and PC later this month. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Complete MLB The Show 26 Trophy List—How To Get All Achievements & Trophies
item.content:encoded:
A new set of innings is upon players with the release of MLB The Show 26, which gives players a new set of achievements and trophies to work towards to boast 100% completion.

MLB The Show 26 releases worldwide on March 16, though early access is available right now for players who splash out. Whether your game mode is Franchise or Road to the Show, there’s plenty to do.

If you’re on the hunt for details on all the achievements and trophies there are to earn in MLB The Show 26, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All MLB The Show 26 Trophies & Achievements

MLB The Show 26 has plenty of trophies and achievements to earn, although the full list is smaller than other games in the sport genre.

The tasks required range from the basic, like swinging the bat for the first time and playing a game in multiplayer, to the more advanced, like getting a Hit or Strikeout when playing on GOAT difficulty.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is MLB The Show 26 on Xbox Game Pass?

Tasks are also spread across various different game modes, and while some wioll be unlocked through natural progression, others will require more dedicated focus.

The table below displays the full list of trophies and achievements to earn in MLB The Show 26.

How Many Achievements & Trophies in MLB The Show 26?

There are 27 trophies and achievements to earn in MLB The Show 26, including the coveted Platinum Trophy on PlayStation for earning all other trophies.

None of the trophies and achievements are hidden or secret, so you can immediately begin to plan out your tasks as you tick them off one by one.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Get Now & Later Packs in MLB The Show 26

Earning all the trophies and achievements will still require some effort, however, as various game modes must be played, including Franchise, Road to the Show, and online.

Are you excited for MLB The Show 26? Which team are you planning to play with? Share your plans in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more MLB The Show 26, find out how you can play in early access, and check out the exact release date and start time details.

item.title: Marvel MaXimum Collection Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Limited Run Games has announced that Marvel MaXimum Collection will release for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, and PC on March 27, 2026.

The announcement for Marvel MaXimum Collection came at IGN Fan Fest 2026 last month, where the developers revealed that it will bring several classic 8-bit and 16-bit games to players, along with features that make these titles more playable on current-generation consoles and systems.

Marvel MaXimum Collection Releases in March 2026

A new post on the official Limited Run Games X account (formerly Twitter) reveals that the Marvel MaXimum Collection will be released digitally first on March 27, 2026, at 10:00 AM EDT. Players can start placing pre-orders for the physical edition of the title on the same day and continue until May 24, 2026. The collection includes 13 iconic titles across consoles and handheld releases, and players can learn more about them below:

About Marvel MaXimum Collection

First up is X-Men: The Arcade Game (Arcade), which now supports full online multiplayer for 6 players, with a rollback netcode. Next up is Captain America and The Avengers, which has an Arcade, MEGA, and 8-bit version. Then we have Spider-Man/Venom: Maximum Carnage, which has a SUPER and a MEGA edition; the former features rich colors, while the latter has a gritty vibe.

Then we have Venom/Spider-Man: Separation Anxiety, which also has a SUPER and MEGA version. The penultimate title is Spider-Man/X-Men: Arcade’s Revenge, which has SUPER, MEGA, Portable, and Gear editions. Last but not least is Silver Surfer, which only has an 8-bit version.

Marvel MaXimum Collection New Features

Coming to the MaXimum features that make the games more playable on current gen, the X-Men: The Arcade Game’s support for full online multiplayer for 6 players and rollback netcode has already been mentioned. Players also get a feature called Archive that lets them check out high-resolution scans of original box art, instructional manuals, and advertisements. There’s a music player that lets you listen to scores for every version of every game.

Marvel MaXimum Collection also comes with an integrated cheat menu that enables features like Infinite Lives and more in select games. Players also get features like Rewind and Save States. Lastly, there are various display options.

In other news, NetEase has issued a warning on social media against deliberately throwing or disrupting matches in Marvel Rivals. Additionally, Insomniac Games has finally revealed the release date for Marvel’s Wolverine. Will you be purchasing Marvel MaXimum Collection when it releases this month? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Control and Alan Wake 2 receiving support for PS5 Pro’s upgraded PSSR. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Control and Alan Wake 2 To Get Upgraded PSSR Support
item.content:encoded:
Remedy Entertainment has announced that Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 will support PS5 Pro’s upgraded PSSR.

The upgraded version of PlayStation 5 Pro’s PSSR was announced late last month, with Sony revealing that Resident Evil Requiem will be the first title to use the feature. More titles will start supporting the upgraded PSSR from March 2026, and now the next two games have been revealed.

Remedy Announces Upgraded PSSR Support for Control and Alan Wake 2

A new post on the official Remedy Entertainment X account (formerly Twitter) reveals that Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 will receive support for the “advanced version of PSSR” soon. However, no date was revealed. Additionally, the post states that players will be able to look forward to “noticeable improvements in image stability clarity of motion.” Players will get to explore “the Oldest House and Bright Falls with a new level of visual fidelity.”

For those wondering about the upgraded PSSR, it is derived from Sony and AMD’s co-developed technology, Project Amethyst. It will provide the very latest from this technology, along with a new settings tool called  ‘Enhance PSSR Image Quality’. Through the new tool, players can check out the upgraded PSSR for any compatible game, with some titles bringing “noticeably crisper graphics.”

In other news, PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics Catalog titles have been officially confirmed for March 2026. Sony is no longer releasing single-player PlayStation exclusives on PC. What are your thoughts on Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 receiving support for PS5 Pro’s upgraded PSSR? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Cyberpunk 2077 devs having no plans to make any additional DLCs or expansions for the game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
